category_title: Students with ASD preoccupied with neatness and accuracy
minimal_context: The student is completing the x20 math game on a Nintendo DS using a stylus while a timer runs on the desk.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please start the x20 math game on your Nintendo DS. I am setting the timer for the session.

STUDENT: (Watches the timer on the desk, then slowly picks up the stylus to write the first answer very carefully.)

TUTOR: Try the next math problem.

STUDENT: (Takes a long time writing the number, trying to make it perfectly neat on the touch screen.)

TUTOR: Keep going.

STUDENT: (Looks distracted, writes an incorrect answer, and immediately restarts the entire x20 game.)

options.A: Invest time focusing on classroom environmental factors, the clarification of expectations, daily routines, and ensuring a structured program during the intervention period.
options.B: Create a nurturing, structured, and responsive environment through interactions and the physical classroom setting to be maintained during the intervention period.
options.C: Blur boundaries between math and other subjects to focus on integrated themes and co-construct a shared sense of morality during the intervention period.
options.D: Incorporate relevant IEP objectives, such as handwriting speed, into a checklist that can be monitored during the intervention period.
